title: gmelina lecomtei
intro: Gmelina lecomtei, also known as the Borneo mahogany or lecomte's gmelina, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is a tall tree that is native to Southeast Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, headache, and rheumatism. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and protecting against oxidative stress. Gmelina lecomtei is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
